name: JOUBERT SYNDROME

demographics.Path: (1)  nearly total aplasia of cerebellar vermis

discriminating_features: "molar tooth sign" on AXIAL image (virtually PATHOGNOMONIC ):

general: "molar tooth sign" on AXIAL image (virtually PATHOGNOMONIC ):
elongated thickened horizontally oriented superior cerebellar peduncles
change in shape of 4th ventricle: triangle-shaped at mid-level + bat-wing-shaped superiorly
cerebellar hemispheres appose one another in midline
superior cerebellar peduncles surrounded by CSF
small isthmus + midbrain in AP diameter ← absence of decussation of superior cerebellar
callosal dysgenesis, cephaloceles, hippocampal malrotation, migrational disorders,

clinical.Prognosis: renal + hepatic disease responsible for mortality
